# NEXT — acme-todo API hardening
<!-- generated by /ratchet-recommend from ratchet/BACKLOG.md, 2026-07-07 -->

> **The loop does not execute this file — you do.** Each wave below gives the exact command to
> paste; run them in order. Regenerate this plan after every loop run (`/ratchet-recommend`).

## ▶ Start here

```
/ratchet-loop --only PERF-03 --verify inline
```

PERF-03 is the safest high-value move: not sensitive, every criterion runnable, no dependencies.
While that runs, answer the UX-05 question below (Wave 1 — it executes later, but a plan that
hides its blockers stalls).

Note: SEC-02 sits at `in-progress` with no run in flight — debris from a crashed session. The
loop's preflight resets it to `todo` automatically; it is routed below as if `todo`.

## Decisions needed from you

- **UX-05 — validation error wording:** users currently see raw zod paths (`body.title: Required`).
  Pick one: (a) generic "Please check your input" + field list, (b) per-field friendly messages
  (needs copy), or (c) keep raw errors for the API and fix display in the client. Answering this
  unblocks UX-05 — then unpark it: journal the decision, remove `[USER-DECISION]`, replace the
  provisional criterion with a real one, set status → `todo`.

## Waves

| # | ID | Lane | Who | Verify | Why | Invocation |
|---|----|------|-----|--------|-----|------------|
| 1 | UX-05 | DECIDE-FIRST | human answers **now**; executes later | — | `[USER-DECISION]`: wording not decided; criterion is provisional — surfacing it first keeps it off the critical path | answer the question above, unpark, then `/ratchet-recommend` |
| 2 | PERF-03 | AUTONOMOUS | cheap model (e.g. Sonnet — `/model sonnet`), unattended | inline | not sensitive; every AC is a runnable backticked command | `/ratchet-loop --only PERF-03 --verify inline` |
| 3 | SEC-02 | SUPERVISED | cheap model allowed, clean-room verified | fresh | Evidence mentions auth/ownership → *sensitive*; Spec names 1 file, no migration → *small* | `/ratchet-loop --only SEC-02 --verify fresh` |
| 4 | OPS-04 | OPS | human/ops | — | `[OPS]`: needs staging server access to rotate the credential | rotate on staging and confirm the old password fails **yourself**; at unpark, rewrite that criterion as human-verified (Human transitions step 3) so every remaining criterion is loop-runnable; then remove `[OPS]`, journal it, status → `todo`, and run `/ratchet-loop --only OPS-04 --verify fresh` for the code half |

## Do NOT automate

- **OPS-04** — the rotation itself needs staging credentials no loop has; a cheap model "completing"
  this would mean marking a live leaked password handled when it isn't.
- **UX-05** — product copy is a human call; any model would be inventing your voice.
